process_name: Rare leptonic decay D0 -> mu+ mu-
implementation_difficulty_reason: D0 -> mu+ mu- is a new c -> u dilepton mode requiring a new Delta C = 1 Wilson coefficient normalization plus long-distance subtraction; it is not covered by the existing Delta F = 2 backend.

commands: rg -n "epsilon_K|epsilon_k|Delta m_K|DELTA_M_K|evaluate_delta_mk|B_d|b_d|B_s|b_s|D0|d_mix|mu.?e.?gamma|muToEGamma|BR_LIMIT|MEG" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "b.?->.?s|X_s|K\\*|R_K|R_D|B_s.*mu|pi.*nu|nu.*nubar|epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|t.?->|Z.?->|h.?->|EDM|dipole|conversion|tau.?->|mu.?->.?3e" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "D0.*mu|D\\^0.*mu|D0.*mumu|mu\\+.*mu|muon.*muon|c.?->.?u|Delta.?C|rare.?charm|rare_decay|C_10|C10|C_S|C_P|pseudoscalar|scalar.*operator" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
evidence: Focused C004 searches found no D0 -> mu+ mu- or Delta C = 1 rare leptonic observable implementation in the required implementation/test directories.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:256 and quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:941 are D0 mixing hits, not D0 -> mu+ mu-.
flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:75 is the separate mu -> e gamma LFV dipole check, not a charm dimuon decay.

status_history.reason: Initial PKA source extraction and C004 draft deposit.

issues: CHK-4: Missing WRITER-DONE status from a WA; latest pre-checker status was WRITER-INITIATED, and no WA batch worklog for C004 was found.
CHK-6: implementation_difficulty is MEDIUM, but the rubric classifies this as HIGH because a live constraint needs a new Delta C = 1 rare-leptonic mode calculation and Wilson normalization.

status_history.reason: Cycle-2 WA rework addressed CA CHK-4 and CHK-6.
Cycle-2 checker re-check passed CHK-1 through CHK-8.
Opus round 1 per-process sign-off; CA-verified PASS retained; see flavor_catalog/signoff/round_001_index.md.

status_history.notes: PDG, CMS, LHCb, and quoted long-distance theory values verified.

status_history.note: B: down-sector alignment promotes up-sector observables

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} \(\mathcal{B}(D^0\to\mu^+\mu^-)\).
This entry covers the rare neutral-charm leptonic decay
\(D^0\to\mu^+\mu^-\), a \(\Delta C=1\), \(c\to u\ell^+\ell^-\)
flavor-changing neutral-current test.  It is distinct from C001/C002
neutral-charm mixing and from semileptonic rare-charm modes because the final
state is purely leptonic and directly probes axial-vector, scalar, and
pseudoscalar short-distance operators.
sections.pdg_prose:
The current PDG Live/API entry S032.28, accessed on 2026-05-16, gives the
headline limit
\[
  \mathcal{B}(D^0\to\mu^+\mu^-) < 2.1\times10^{-9}
  \qquad (90\%~{\rm C.L.}).
\]
The same PDG entry notes that the underlying CMS 2025 analysis reports the
95\% C.L. limit
\[
  \mathcal{B}(D^0\to\mu^+\mu^-) < 2.4\times10^{-9}.
\]
These values are traced to
\texttt{flavor\_catalog/references/C004/pdg2026\_d0\_mumu\_pdgLive.txt}
and \texttt{cms2025\_d0\_mumu\_public.txt}.  The requested LHCb predecessor
used \(9\,{\rm fb}^{-1}\) at 7, 8, and 13 TeV and set
\(\mathcal{B}(D^0\to\mu^+\mu^-)<3.1\times10^{-9}\) at 90\% C.L.,
with a 95\% C.L. companion limit of \(3.5\times10^{-9}\)
(\texttt{lhcb2023\_arxiv2212\_11203}).
sections.relevance:
Anarchic warped models can generate flavor-changing \(c\to u\) neutral-current
couplings through KK gauge exchange, electroweak \(Z\)-like effects, Higgs or
radion scalar exchange, or lepton-sector extensions.  This channel is therefore
a useful up-sector \(\Delta C=1\) complement to the implemented \(\Delta F=2\)
D-mixing constraint.  For the present quark-only scan it is best treated as a
catalog and roadmap entry; a live constraint would require lepton-current
normalization in addition to the quark flavor spurions.
sections.post_2008:
Since the arXiv:0804.1954 warped-flavor baseline, the experimental bound has
moved by orders of magnitude.  LHCb's full Run 1+2 search improved the rare
charm dimuon limit to \(3.1\times10^{-9}\) at 90\% C.L.  CMS then used the
Run 3 inclusive dimuon-trigger program, \(64.5\,{\rm fb}^{-1}\) from
2022--2023, to set the stronger 95\% C.L. limit of \(2.4\times10^{-9}\).
On the theory side, the 2024 rare-charm EFT analysis incorporated recent
\(\Delta C=1\) data, including \(D^0\to\mu^+\mu^-\), into constraints on
\(C_{7,9,10}^{(\prime)}\) while emphasizing that null-test observables still
leave room for new physics.
sections.validity:
The experimental limit is robust, but the Standard Model prediction is not a
clean short-distance number.  The classic rare-charm calculation estimates the
long-distance two-photon contribution as
\(\mathcal{B}(D^0\to\mu^+\mu^-)_{\gamma\gamma}
\simeq 2.7\times10^{-5}\mathcal{B}(D^0\to\gamma\gamma)\), implying a
long-distance floor of at least \(3\times10^{-13}\) for the assumptions quoted
there.  The safe catalog use is therefore an upper bound on extra
short-distance contributions, not a precision SM subtraction.

In down-aligned or kaon-protected RS variants, up-sector observables become
leading rather than secondary diagnostics.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{NO.}  The required catalog greps and a focused search for
\texttt{D0.*mu}, \texttt{c.?->.?u}, \texttt{rare.?charm},
\texttt{C10}, \texttt{C\_S}, \texttt{C\_P}, and scalar/pseudoscalar rare-decay
terms found no live \(D^0\to\mu^+\mu^-\) implementation in
\texttt{quarkConstraints/}, \texttt{qcd/}, \texttt{flavorConstraints/},
\texttt{neutrinos/}, \texttt{yukawa/}, \texttt{warpConfig/},
\texttt{solvers/}, \texttt{scanParams/}, or \texttt{tests/}.  Nearby hits are
unrelated: \(D^0\) mixing appears at \texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:256}
and \texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:941}, while
\(\mu\to e\gamma\) is handled separately at
\texttt{flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:75}.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{HIGH.}  \(D^0\to\mu^+\mu^-\) is a new \(c\to u\) dilepton mode
requiring a new \(\Delta C=1\) Wilson-coefficient normalization and a documented
long-distance subtraction; it is not covered by the existing \(\Delta F=2\)
backend.
sections.key_references:
Process-local keys before bibliography consolidation:
\texttt{PDG2026:D0MuMu}, \texttt{CMS2025:D0MuMu},
\texttt{LHCb2023:D0MuMu},
\texttt{BurdmanGolowichHewettPakvasa2002:RareCharm},
\texttt{GisbertHillerSuelmann2024:RareCharmEFT}, and
\texttt{CsakiFalkowskiWeiler2008:CompositeFlavor}.

L21:   CMS summary states this was the most sensitive search to date and improved
L22:   on the previous best result.

L19:   sensitive to D0 mesons from other sources.
L20:   Result: no evidence for an excess above expected background.
L21:   Limit: B(D0 -> mu+ mu-) < 3.1 x 10^-9 at 90% CL.
